Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log of 5 cases against a normative process description I need to identify which cases deviate from the described process and explain the violations based on the text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Step 2 Check inventory must occur next Branch 1 Sufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraints Authorize payment after Check inventory and before packaging Package items after authorization and before shipping Ship order after packaging never after cancellation Close order is always the final activity Branch 2 Insufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order Constraints No fulfillment activities Package Ship after cancellation Close order is final General Constraints Summary Check inventory before Authorize payment or Cancel order Authorize payment before Package items Package items before Ship order Ship order never after Cancel order Close order must be the last activity in all cases Any deviation from these sequences rules is a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close Check against rules Matches Branch 1 exactly All constraints satisfied Close is last No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Check against rules Package items occurs before Authorize payment Constraint violated Package items should only occur after authorization Also Authorize payment should occur before packaging Violation Incorrect order of Package and Authorize

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close Check against rules After Cancel order it proceeds to Ship order Constraint violated Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also fulfillment activities Ship shouldnt happen after cancellation The sequence for cancellation should be Receive Check Cancel Close Here it has extra steps Authorize Ship after cancellation and Ship happens after Cancel Violation Shipping after cancellation inclusion of fulfillment steps after cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close Check against rules Matches Branch 2 exactly All constraints satisfied Close is last No violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Check against rules Package items is completely missing It goes directly from Authorize payment to Ship order Constraint violated Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping The text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Skipping packaging is a violation of the normative flow Violation Missing Package items step before shipping

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Structure Output List each case that violates the process For each violating case explicitly state the violations and reference the normative description Case 1 Compliant Case 2 Violates order of Package vs Authorize Case 3 Violates by shipping after cancellation performing fulfillment after cancellation Case 4 Compliant Case 5 Violates by skipping packaging step

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for ambiguities mentioned in the prompt one might question whether its ever acceptable to authorize payment before certain conditions Ill stick to the explicit constraints given The prompt says Any deviation from these general principles would be considered a violation So strict adherence is expected

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during thought Case 2 Package before Authorize Text says Authorize payment should only occur before packaging Violation confirmed Case 3 Cancel then Ship Text says Ship order should never occur after a cancellation Violation confirmed Case 5 Missing Package Text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Violation confirmed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait lets double check Case 5 Is skipping a step considered a deviation It doesnt explicitly list skipping a step but it does say the expected flow is Receive Check Authorize Payment Package Ship Close Skipping Package breaks the Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order rule So yes its a violation
